text: 🇹🇷 An 18-year-old opened fire at a school in Turkey and then killed himself, local authorities report. At least 18 people were injured, including students and teachers. The attacker used a pump-action shotgun, first shooting in the schoolyard and then inside the building. The incident took place in the city of Siverek in Turkey’s southeastern Şanlıurfa province. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Open Source Intel Kremlin: Israel is playing a "dangerous game" near Bushehr that endangers Russian specialists and risks a "massive nuclear catastrophe" for the Middle East. The IDF reportedly refined its strike coordinates to avoid Russian personnel. Tensions remained high through the days preceding the ceasefire. - Channel 12 tweet

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇺🇸 🤡 U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance stated that Washington has achieved its objectives in Iran and that it is now possible to begin ending the military operation. 🇮🇷 In response to the U.S. naval blockade, Iran threatened to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait — “you will soon nostalgically remember cheap gasoline at $4–5,” said Iran’s parliament speaker. 🟦 Iran has demanded compensation from five Arab countries for their “participation” alongside the U.S. and Israel. At the start of the war, Iran had launched strikes on Bahrain, Jordan, Qatar, the UAE, Saudi Arabia, and other regional states. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇭🇺 What to know about Hungary’s incoming prime minister, Péter Magyar: 📍 Until 2024, he held senior positions in state structures, particularly in the banking sector and EU-related fields. 📍 He was married to Judit Varga, a key figure in Orbán’s government and former justice minister. 📍 His career advancement was largely based on connections within Orbán’s circle. 📍 Inside the system, he was viewed as a confrontational and overly independent figure, which limited his access to top positions. 📍 After his divorce and the loss of political backing, he gradually lost influence and stepped away from key roles. 📍 A turning point came with the 2024 scandal involving a pardon linked to covering up child sexual abuse. Following this, Magyar publicly broke with the system and accused it of corruption, nepotism, abuse of power, and “mafia-style” governance. 📍 He then became one of the main public opponents of the government and took leadership of the Tisza party, which quickly evolved into a leading opposition force. 📍 In foreign policy, he maintains a cautious stance: not openly pro-Ukraine, careful toward the EU and NATO, critical of accelerating Ukraine’s EU membership, while distancing himself from Orbán’s pro-Russian course and not blocking the EU’s €90 billion support initiative for Ukraine. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

Norway’s planned delivery of at least six F-16 jets to Ukraine is facing major delays, with none transferred as of April 2026. Even in the best-case scenario, the jets may not arrive until 2027—far later than the original 2024 timeline. Source: Defense Express
Spain has officially launched a major plan to regularize undocumented migrants.

🇪🇺 "The cheapest energy is the one you don't use." - Reich Gauleitierin von der Leyen 🐻 It's nice that Europeans have strong leadership, that has plans in place for almost everything. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇴🇲 The UKMTO reported an incident on April 7, 2026, about 112 nautical miles southeast of Ras Al Hadd, Oman. A bulk carrier was hit by two unidentified projectiles, causing a fire onboard. A Pakistani naval vessel assisted, and the situation has since been resolved. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🔄 Forwarded from Seyed Mohammad Marandi: My advice to anyone who wants to better understand the Iranian-US relationship during these very challenging times is to read 'Going to Tehran' by Flynt and Hillary Mann Leverett. Both authors served as senior officials in the US National Security Council.

⚡️ 🇷🇺 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Russia’s ambassador to Tehran, Alexey Dedov, said the United States still has not understood that the language of force does not work with Iran. He condemned attacks on Iran and pointed to the Tehran‑Moscow strategic treaty. Dedov added that Iran is defending its rights, and that Washington claims Tehran should not have nuclear weapons while ignoring Iran’s repeated statements that its nuclear programme is peaceful. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇩🇪 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said direct talks between Israel and Lebanon can be an important first step towards respecting both Israel’s legitimate security interests and Lebanon’s territorial integrity. He called for Hezbollah’s attacks on Israel to stop and supported Lebanon’s monopoly on force and Hezbollah’s disarmament. He added that the Israeli army must protect civilians and civilian infrastructure. Germany provided an additional €45 million in humanitarian aid to Lebanon in March. @wfwitness

Lebanon insists on a ceasefire, even temporary, before any talks, calling…" ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Al Araby: The Lebanese government sources said today’s trilateral Lebanese‑American‑Israeli meeting will discuss a ceasefire. Lebanon is counting on U.S. and international pressure on Israel to halt the fire. An Israeli truce declaration is necessary for negotiations to begin. In any future talks, Lebanon insists on a full Israeli withdrawal from all its territory. Simon Karem will head Lebanon’s delegation once a negotiating path is agreed upon. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇦🇺 AFP : Australia says it will boost spending on drones by up to A$5 billion as part of a broader push to adapt to changes in modern warfare. Defense Minister Richard Marles said the move is driven by lessons from conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine, where large numbers of low cost drones and counter drone systems have become central to combat, with Australia planning to invest up to A$15 billion in autonomous capabilities over the next decade. @wfwitness

Xi Jinping to Pedro Sánchez: China and Spain are both principled and reasonable countries. We are willing to stand on the right side of history. We should oppose the law of the jungle and together maintain true multilateralism and protect world peace and development.

🇨🇳 🇮🇷 The Chinese tanker Rich Starry, subject to US sanctions, passed through the Strait of Hormuz despite the blockade imposed by Donald Trump the day before, CNN reports According to MarineTraffic data, the ship with a Malawian flag first attempted to cross the strait on April 13 but was forced to turn back. A new attempt was made in the evening, and by early Tuesday morning, the route was successfully completed. In addition, on Monday, another oil tanker, Elpis, flying the flag of the Comoros Islands, passed through the strait after the blockade came into effect. The vessel was under US sanctions in 2025 for transporting Iranian oil "as part of Iran's shadow fleet". 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 Nidaa Al-Watan: The trilateral meeting of Israeli, Lebanese and U.S. ambassadors in Washington represents a decisive stage in setting the framework for direct negotiations. Lebanon insists on a ceasefire, even temporary, before any talks, calling it essential for a balanced process. Israel, however, pushes for negotiations focused on Hezbollah’s disarmament while maintaining military pressure – effectively negotiating under fire. A Lebanese presidential source rejected Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem’s call to cancel the meeting, stating that Lebanon is trying to stop the bloodshed and repair the mistake of others (Hezbollah) who launched the war. Lebanon is not making concessions but defending its sovereignty. A Lebanese government source said the U.S. has committed to pressuring Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to agree to a ceasefire in southern Lebanon. The meeting’s success hinges on securing such a ceasefire, which would then allow broader talks. @wfwitness

🇷🇺 An SBU agent who was transmitting data about the objects of the Black Sea Fleet has been detained in Crimea The FSB also detained three participants in the preparation of a terrorist attack against a high-ranking security official in Moscow 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇨🇳 🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 A Chinese tanker sanctioned by the United States passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday despite a U.S. blockade on the chokepoint, shipping data showed. The Rich Starry would be the first to make it through the strait and to exit the Persian Gulf since the blockade began, data from LSEG, Marine Traffic and Kpler showed.
